name: Oxytocin
subtitle: Neurohypophysial Peptide | Social Bonding & Reproductive Hormone
overview: Nine-amino-acid peptide hormone produced in hypothalamus, released by posterior pituitary. Functions in social bonding, trust, empathy, reproduction, childbirth, lactation. FDA-approved synthetically for labor induction and postpartum hemorrhage.
mechanism: Binds oxytocin receptors on uterine smooth muscle, triggering calcium influx and myometrial contractions. Stimulates prostaglandin release. In CNS, modulates GABAergic, serotonergic, dopaminergic neurotransmission; reduces cortisol and HPA axis activity.
keyBenefits: FDA-approved for labor induction with precise dosing via IV
Established safety profile in clinical use
Researched for anxiety, PTSD, autism, sexual dysfunction

items.name: Labor Induction
items.description: Initiates or augments uterine contractions when vaginal delivery medically indicated.

items.name: Postpartum Hemorrhage Control
items.description: Controls postpartum bleeding through uterine contraction stimulation during third stage.

items.name: Incomplete Abortion Management
items.description: Adjunctive therapy for incomplete or inevitable abortion in second trimester.

items.name: Autism Spectrum Disorders
items.description: Extensive research with mixed results on social functioning; optimal dosing unclear.

items.name: PTSD Augmentation
items.description: Combined with exposure therapy; shows reduced PTSD/depression symptoms.

items.name: Social Anxiety
items.description: Decreases amygdala reactivity to social threats; normalizes brain connectivity.

items.name: Sexual Function
items.description: Improves libido, arousal, orgasm intensity in both sexes.

deliveryMethods.overview: IV or IM administration in clinical settings. IV provides immediate onset; requires continuous infusion and monitoring for labor use.

protocols.dose: 0.5-2 mU/min initial, titrate to 1-2 mU/min every 30-60 min
10 units IM or 10-40 units in IV solution

materials: Compatible IV solution (0.9% NaCl or Lactated Ringer's)
steps: Dilute oxytocin in compatible IV solution per facility protocol
Use infusion pump for precise rate control
Monitor uterine contractions and fetal heart rate continuously

deliveryMethods.overview: Nasal spray delivers oxytocin directly to brain via olfactory and trigeminal pathways, bypassing blood-brain barrier. Primary research route for psychological/social effects.

protocols.frequency: Once daily or before social situations

materials: Oxytocin nasal spray (compounded) or lyophilized powder
steps: If using pre-made compounded spray, proceed to use
For powder: calculate desired concentration (typically 4-6 IU per 0.1mL)
Add calculated volume of sterile saline to vial
Gently swirl until fully dissolved—do not shake
Transfer to sterile metered-dose nasal spray bottle
Prime pump with 2-3 sprays before first use

deliveryMethods.overview: Sublingual troches provide needle-free administration. Lower bioavailability (~4.5% vs 11% intranasal) requires higher doses.

interactions.notes: Structurally similar; overlapping receptor affinity with potential additive effects.
Complementary anxiolytic effects—Selank modulates GABA; oxytocin acts on central receptors.
Complementary sexual function effects via different pathways.
Both involved in reproduction; kisspeptin stimulates GnRH; oxytocin enhances arousal.
No direct interactions; different mechanisms (tissue repair vs social function).
SSRIs may affect endogenous oxytocin; combined use requires healthcare provider oversight.
Alcohol suppresses oxytocin release; reduces therapeutic effects.

timeline.effects: IV: 1-3 minute onset, 30-60 minute duration; IM: 3-5 minute onset
15-30 minute onset, peak 30-60 minutes, 2-4 hour duration
30-45 minute onset, peak ~60 minutes, 2-4 hour duration

stopSigns: Signs of water intoxication (confusion, seizures, swelling)
monitoring: Continuous electronic fetal monitoring during labor (injectable)
Electrolyte levels if exceeding 24 IU daily long-term (intranasal)

good: FDA-approved pharmaceutical product from licensed pharmacies (injectable)
Clear, colorless solution without particles or discoloration
Proper packaging with verified lot number, expiration, concentration
Licensed compounding pharmacy source with USP compliance
warning: Sublingual has lower bioavailability than intranasal
bad: Improper storage or broken cold chain

references.title: Intranasal Oxytocin in Autism Spectrum Disorder
references.keyFindings: N=290 children/adolescents; 48 IU daily; 24 weeks. Did not significantly improve social functioning vs placebo; highlights need for better patient selection.

references.title: Oxytocin Augmented Prolonged Exposure Therapy for PTSD
references.keyFindings: Veterans with PTSD; 40 IU intranasal combined with PE therapy. Lower PTSD and depression symptoms; higher therapeutic alliance vs placebo.

references.title: Intranasal Oxytocin for Obesity
references.keyFindings: N=61 adults with obesity; 24 IU four times daily; 8 weeks. No weight loss but significant reduction in caloric intake; improved mental health-related quality of life.

quickStats.typicalDose: Intranasal: 20-24 IU; Injectable: 10-40 IU; Sublingual: 50-100 IU
quickStats.frequency: As needed for social/sexual support, or 1-2x daily for anxiety/PTSD protocols; max 1 dose per 24hr for sexual function
quickStats.cycleDuration: As needed for research applications; 4-12 weeks for anxiety/autism protocols
